title: בִּרְכַּת גּוֹמֵל חֲסָדִים טוֹבִים לְעַמּוֹ יִשְׂרָאֵל | Birkat Gomel Ḥasadim and prayers of protection following the Birkhot ha-Shaḥar in the Italian rite
author: Aharon N. Varady (transcription)

coauthors.name: Aharon N. Varady (transcription)
coauthors.bio: Aharon Varady (M.A.J.Ed./JTSA Davidson) is a volunteer transcriber for the Open Siddur Project. If you find any mistakes in his transcriptions, please let him know. Shgiyot mi yavin; Ministarot naqeni שְׁגִיאוֹת מִי־יָבִין; מִנִּסְתָּרוֹת נַקֵּנִי "Who can know all one's flaws? From hidden errors, correct me" (Psalms 19:13). If you'd like to directly support his work, please consider donating via his Patreon account. (Varady also translates prayers and contributes his own original work besides serving as the primary shammes of the Open Siddur Project and its website, opensiddur.org.)

coauthors.name: Aharon N. Varady (translation)
coauthors.bio: Aharon Varady (M.A.J.Ed./JTSA Davidson) is a volunteer translator for the Open Siddur Project. If you find any mistakes in his translations, please let him know. Shgiyot mi yavin; Ministarot Naqeni שְׁגִיאוֹת מִי־יָבִין; מִנִּסְתָּרוֹת נַקֵּנִי "Who can know all one's flaws? From hidden errors, correct me" (Psalms 19:13). If you'd like to directly support his work, please consider donating via his Patreon account. (Varady also transcribes prayers and contributes his own original work besides serving as the primary shammes for the Open Siddur Project and its website, opensiddur.org.)

coauthors.name: David Prato (translation)
coauthors.bio: David Prato (1882–1951) was an Italian rabbi and Jewish leader in the early days of the State of Israel. Born in Leghorn, Prato was chief cantor in Florence, rabbi of Alexandria (Egypt) from 1927 to 1936, and chief rabbi of Rome from 1936 to 1938. Conditions became impossible for him when the Fascist regime began its antisemitic policy, and Prato moved to Erets Yisrael. He resumed his post in Rome in 1945. Prato played a prominent part in the administration of the Jewish National Fund and Keren Hayesod in Italy. In 1929 he founded in Alexandria the French-Hebrew review L'Illustration Juive, which was followed by Cahiers Juifs in 1933. He published a prayerbook, Tefilah l'David (1949) and two collections of sermons, Cinque anni di Rabbinato (1933), and Dal pergamo della Comunità di Roma (1950), covering his activities in Rome.

coauthors.name: Unknown
coauthors.bio: Sometimes the best we can do in attributing a historical work is to indicate the period and place it was written, the first prayer book it may have been printed in, or the archival collection in which the manuscript was found. We invite the public to help to attribute all works to their original composers. If you know something not mentioned in the commentary offered, please leave a comment or contact us.

excerpt: This is the form of the prophylactic prayers following the birkhot hashaḳar and which precede the birkhot Talmud Torah in the Italian nusaḥ attested by Rabbi David Prato from his siddur, תפלה לדוד Preghiere di Rito Italiano (1949), pp. 10-12.
content:
Source (Hebrew) Translation (Italian) Translation (English)
בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה
יְיָ אֱלֹהֵנוּ
מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם
הַמַּעֲבִיר חֶבְלֵי שֵׁנָה מֵעֵינָי
וּתְנוּמָה מֵעַפְעַפָּי׃
Benedetto Tu,
o Eterno Dio
nostro Re dell’universo
che sciogliesti i lacci del sonno dai miei occhi
e la sonnolenza dalle mie palpebre.
Blessed are you,
Hashem our Èlōăhʰ,
Cosmic majesty,
who removes the bonds of sleep from my eyes
and drowsiness from my eyelids.
יְהִי רָצוֹן מִלְּפָנֶיךָ
יְיָ אֱלֹהַי וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתַי
שֶׁתַּרְגִּילֵנִי לִדְבַר מִצְוָה
וְאַל תַּרְגִּילֵנִי לִדְבַר עֲבֵרָה וְעָוֺן
וְהַשְׁלֵט בִּי יֵצֶר טוֹב
וְאַל תַּשְׁלֵט בִּי יֵצֶר רָע
וְחַזְּקֵנִי בְּמִצְוֺתֶיךָ
וְתֵן חֶלְקִי בְּתוֹרָתֶךָ
וְאַל תְּבִיאֵנִי לִידֵי חֵטְא
וְלֹא לִידֵי נִסָּיוֹן
וְלֹא לִידֵי בִזָּיֹן
וְכוֹף אֶת יִצְרִי לְהִשְׁתַּעְבֵּד לָךְ
וְהַכְנַע עָרְפִּי לָשׁוּב אֵלֶיךָ
וְחַדֵּשׁ כִּלְיוֹתַי לִשְׁמוֹר פִּקּוּדֶיךָ
וּמוֹל אֶת לְבָבִי וְאֶת לְבַב זַרְעִי
לְאַהֲבָה וּלְיִרְאָה אֶת שְׁמֶךָ
וְלַעֲשׂוֹת הַטּוֹב וְהַיָּשָׁר בְּעֵינֶיךָ
וְקָֽרְבֵנִי לְכׇל מַה שֶּׁאָהַבְתָּ
וְרַחֲקֵנִי מִכׇּל מַה שֶּׁשָּׂנֵאתָ׃
Ti piaccia
o Eterno mio Dio e Dio dei miei padri
di abituarmi ad opere buone
e non alla traspressione e al peccato,
fà che io sia dominato dal bene
e non dall’impulso che spinge al male,
che mi fortifichi nei Tuoi precetti
e che m’interessi di continuo alla Tua legge.
Non lasciarmi cadere in peccato,
non mi esporre alla tentazione e al disprezzo,
piega la mia inclinazione in modo che si assoggetti al Tuo dominio.
Umilia la mia caparbietà facendo a Te ritorno,
rinnova l’intimo della mia coscienza
in modo che osservi i Tuoi comandi,
correggi il mio cuore e quello della mia discendenza
per amare, per temere il Tuo nome
e per eseguire tutto quanto è bene e retto dinnanzi ai Tuoi occhi.
Avvicinami a tutto quanto Tu ami
e allontanami da tutto quanto Tu odi
May it please you,
Hashem my Èlōăhʰ and Èlōăhʰ of my ancestors,
to accustom me to good deeds
and not to transgression and sin;
grant that I may be ruled by goodness
and not by the impulse that drives me to evil;
strengthen me in your precepts
and keep me constantly attentive to your Torah.
Do not let me fall into sin;
do not expose me to temptation and contempt;
bend my inclination so that it may submit to your dominion.
Humble my stubbornness by returning me to you;
renew the depths of my conscience
so that I may observe your commands;
Set my heart and the hearts of my descendants aright,
that we may love you, fear your name,
and do all that is good and right in your sight.
Draw me near to all that you love
and turn me away from all that you hate.
וְקַיֵם עָלַי
וְעַל זַרְעִי וְעַל זֶרע זַרְעִי
מִקְרָא שְֶכָּתוּב
וּמָל יְיָ אֶלֹהֶיךָ אֶת לְבָֽבְךָ
וְאֶת לְבַב זַרְעֶךָ
לְאַהֲבָה אֶת יְיָ אֱלֹהֶיךָ
בְּכׇל לְבָֽבְךָ
וּבְכׇל נַפְשְׁךָ
לְמַעַן חַיֶּיךָ׃ (דברים ל:ו)
e mantieni in favor mio
in favore di tutta la mia tarda posterità
quanto è scritto nella Torà:
«l’Eterno tuo Dio correggerà il tuo cuore
e il cruore della tua discendenza
per amarlo
con tutto il tuo cuore
e con tutta la tua anima
affinchè tu viva la vera vita.» (Deuteronomy 30:6)
And in my favor, grant me—
and in favor of all my descendants after me—
what is written in the Torah:
“Hashem your Èlōăhʰ will circumcise your heart
and the heart of your seed,
to love Hashem your Èlōăhʰ
with all your heart
and with all your being,
in order that you may live.” (Deuteronomy 30:6)
וַאֲנִי זֹאת בְּרִיתִי אוֹתָם
אָמַר יְיָ רוּחִי אֲשֶׁר עָלֶיךָ וּדְבָרַי
אֲשֶׁר שַׂמְתִּי בְּפִיךָ
לֹא יָמוּשׁוּ מִפִּיךָ
וּמִפִּי זַרְעֲךָ
וּמִפִּי זֶרַע זַרְעֲךָ
אָמַר יְיָ מֵעַתָּה וְעַד עוֹלָם: (ישעיה נט:כא)
«Questo io prometto loro, dice l’Eterno,
il mio spirito che è sopra di te
e le mie parole che ho posto sulla tua bocca
non si allontaneranno giammai dalle tue labbra
ed a quelle della tua
tarda posterità,
dice l’Eterno». (Isaiah 59:21)
“As for me, this is my covenant with them, says Hashem;
my spirit that is upon you,
and my words which I have put in your mouth,
shall not depart out of your mouth,
nor out of the mouth of your children,
nor out of the mouth of your children’s progeny,
says Hashem, from henceforth and for ever.” (Isaiah 59:21)
וְנוֹדַע בַּגּוֹיִם זַרְעָם
וְצֶאֱצָאֵיהֶם בְּתוֹךְ הָעַמִּים
כׇּל רֹאֵיהֶם יַכִּירוּם
כִּי הֵם זֶרַע
בֵּרַךְ יְיָ׃ (ישעיה סא:ט)
«Si distingueranno fra i popoli i figli d’Israele
e i loro discendenti fra tutte le nazioni:
chiunque li vedrà constaterà
che essi sono una discendenza degna
della benedizione dell’Eterno». (Isaiah 61:9)
“The children of Yisrael will stand out among the peoples,
and their descendants among all nations;
whoever sees them will recognize
that they are a people worthy
of Hashem’s blessing.” (Isaiah 61:9)
לֹא יִגְעוּ לָרִיק
וְלֹא יֵלְדוּ לַבֶּהָלָה
כִּי זֶרַע בְּרוּכֵי יְיָ הֵמָּה
וְצֶאֱצָאֵיהֶם אִתָּם׃
וְהָיָה טֶרֶם יִקְרָאוּ וַאֲנִי אֶעֱנֶה
עוֹד הֵם מְדַבְּרִים
וַאֲנִי אֶשְׁמָע׃ (ישעיה סה:כג-כד)
«Non faticheranno invano
e non saranno nati inutilmente poichè essi,
e quelli che scaturiranno da loro,
formeranno una discendenza benedetta da Dio.
Io risponderò loro prima che mi invochino
e mentre stanno per parlare
Io li esaudirò». (Isaiah 65:23-24)
“They will not labor in vain,
nor will they have been born in vain,
for they—and those who come from them—
will form a people blessed by Hashem.
I will answer them before they call upon me,
and while they are still speaking,
I will hear them.” (Isaiah 65:23-24)
כִּי אֶצָּק־מַיִם עַל צָמֵא
וְנוֹזְלִים עַל יַבָּשָׁה
אֶצֹּק רוּחִי עַל זַרְעֶךָ
וּבִרְכָתִי עַל צֶאֱצָאֶיךָ׃
וְצָֽמְחוּ בְּבֵין חָצִיר
כַּעֲרִבִים עַל יִבְלֵי מָיִם׃
זֶה יֹאמַר לַיְיָ אָנִי
וְזֶה יִקְרָא בְשֵׁם יַעֲקֹב
וְזֶה יִכְתֹּב יָדוֹ לַיְיָ
וּבְשֵׁם יִשְׂרָאֵל יְכַנֶּה׃ (ישעיה מד:ג-ה)
«Come Io verso la pioggia su un terreno sitibondo
e acque correnti sull’asciutto
così Io verserò il mio spirito sulla sua discendenza
e la mia benedizione sulla sua prole.
Cresceranno come l’erba,
come i salici presso i torrenti d’acqua.
L’uno affermerà di appartenere all’Eterno,
l’altro invocherà il nome di Giacobbe
e l’altro si firmerà servo del Signore
e si chiamerà col nome d’Israele». (Isaiah 44:3-5)
“As I pour rain upon parched ground
and flowing waters upon the dry land,
so I will pour out my spirit upon their descendants
and my blessing upon their offspring.
They will grow like grass,
like willows by flowing streams.
One will claim to belong to Hashem,
another will invoke the name of Yaaqov,
and another will call himself a servant of Hashem
and take the name of Yisrael.” (Isaiah 44:3-5)
וַיְהִי כַחוֹל זַרְעֶךָ
וְצֶאֱצָאֵי מֵאֶיךָ
כִּמְעוֹתָיו
לֹא יִכָּרֵת
וְלֹא יִשָּׁמֵד
שְׁמוֹ מִלְפָנָי׃ (ישעיה מח:יט)
«Come la sabbia sarà numerosa la tua discendenza,
come i suoi granelli coloro
che usciranno dalle tue viscere.
Il suo nome non si cancellerà
e non sarà mai distrutto
dinnanzi a me». (Isaiah 48:19)
“Your descendants will be as numerous as the sand,
and those who come from your loins
will be as countless as its grains.
His name will not be cut off,
nor will it ever be destroyed
before me.” (Isaiah 48:19)
וְתִהְיֶה לִי לְמִשְׂגָּב וּמִבְטָח וּמִגְדַּל עוֹז
וּבֵית מְצוּדוֹת לְהוֹשִׁיעֵנִי וּלְהַצִּיל נַפְשִׁי מִשַּׁחַת
וּמִכׇּל צָרָה
וּתְנֵנִי הַיוֹם וּבְכׇל יוֹם
לְחֵן וּלְחֶסֶד וּלְרַחֲמִים בְּעֵינֶיךָ
וּבְעֵינֵי כׇּל רוֹאַי
וְגָמְלֵנִי חַסָדִים טוֹבִים׃
בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְיָ
גּוֹמֵל חֲסָדִים טוֹבִים
לְעַמּוֹ יִשְׂרָאֵל׃
Sii per me di asilo, di riparo e di forza
per salvarmi e per liberarmi dal sepolcro
e da qualsiasi angoscia.
Dammi quest’oggi e in ogni giorno
grazia, misericordia e pietà dinnanzi ai tuoi occhi
e dinnanzi a tutti coloro che guardano a me
e usami le più grandi misericordie.
Benedetto Tu o Eterno,
che usa le più grandi misericordie
verso il suo popolo d’Israele,
amen.
Be my refuge, my shelter, and my strength
to save me and deliver me from the grave
and from all distress.
Grant me today and every day
grace, mercy, and compassion in your sight
and in the sight of all who look upon me,
and show me your greatest mercy.
Blessed are you, Hashem,
who shows the greatest mercy
toward your people of Yisrael.
Amen.
יְהִי רָצוֹן מִלְּפָנֶיךָ
יְיָ אֱלֹהַי אֲבוֹתַי
שֶׁתַּצִּילֵנִי הַיּוֹם וּבְכׇל יוֹם
מֵעַזֵּי פָנִים וּמֵעַזּוּת פָּנִים
וּמִיֵּצֶר הָרָע
וּמֵאָדָם רָע
וּמִשָּׁכֵן רָע
וּמִלָּשׁוֹן הָרָע
וּמִשָּׂטָן הַמַּשְׁחִית
וּמִדִּין קָשֶׁה
וּמִבַּעַל דִּין קָשֶׁה
בֵּין שֶׁהוּא בֶן בְּרִית
וּבֵין שֶׁאֵינוֹ בֶן בְּרִית
וּמִדִּינָהּ שֶׁל גֵיהִנָּם׃
Ti piaccia
o Eterno mio Dio e Dio dei miei padri
di preservarmi in ogni giorno
della mia esistenza dagli insolenti e dall’insolenza,
dalla passione malvagia,
dall’uomo perverso,
dal vicino pericoloso,
dalla calunnia,
dalla seduzione,
da una sentenza ingiusta
e da un caparbio litigante
a qualunque
popolo appartenga
e dal destino infernale.
May it please you,
Hashem my Èlōăhʰ and Èlōăhʰ of my ancestors,
to protect me every day of my life
from the insolent and from insolence,
from malevolent passion,
from the wicked man,
from the dangerous neighbor,
from slander,
from seduction,
from an unjust judgment,
and from a stubborn litigant,
whatever people
he may belong to,
and from a hellish fate.
continue with the blessing over learning Torah

This is the form of the prophylactic prayers following the birkhot hashaḳar and which precede the birkhot Talmud Torah in the Italian nusaḥ attested by Rabbi David Prato from his siddur, תפלה לדוד Preghiere di Rito Italiano (1949), pp. 10-12. The Italian translation is from Rabbi Prato. The English translation is based on the Italian, with re-Hebraizations of Divine and personal names and hewing closer to the original Hebrew where elisions were made in the Italian. I have also added scriptural citations. –Aharon Varady

Source(s)

featured_image.title: I Saw Othello’s Visage in His Mind (Fred Wilson 2013)
featured_image.caption: "I Saw Othello's Visage in His Mind" (Fred Wilson 2013)
